key_insight: EquiFiLM adds continuous charge conditioning to equivariant foundation force fields with a simple FiLM block on scalar channels, enabling accurate modeling of driven electronic-state changes without breaking E(3)-equivariance.
engineering_relevance: This lets researchers adapt existing ML force-field backbones to charged, excited, or field-driven materials processes using only a small amount of new DFT data and no inference-time cost increase.
method: Insert per-layer Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM) conditioned on external charge into an equivariant MLFF.
Modulate only scalar interaction channels so rotational and translational equivariance are preserved exactly.
Demonstrate the approach on liquid water using MACE-MatPES as the backbone, producing E-MACE.
evidence_signal: On four training charges, E-MACE cut force RMSE from 21.3 to 6.96 meV/Å and energy RMSE from 6.1 to 0.1 meV/atom, and it remained stable across seven held-out interpolation/extrapolation charges and molecular dynamics tests.
watch_item: The approach still depends on representative labeled data across the conditioning range, so strong extrapolation beyond the sampled charge regime may remain uncertain.
paper_title: EquiFiLM: Charge-Conditioned Equivariant Force Fields via Feature-wise Linear Modulation
